rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydroboration of styrenes with HBpin in CH3CN/THF using DIEA, proceeding via a radical pathway with CH3CN as the primary hydrogen donor to the benzyl position
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of DIEA to an N-centered radical cation, which then oxidizes CH3CN and HBpin to their radical cations; the resulting Bpin radical adds to styrene and the benzyl radical/boronate alkyl intermediate is reduced at the cathode, where H+ is also reduced to H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes DIEA to a radical cation, which abstracts electrons from CH3CN and HBpin, generating •CH2CN and •Bpin after deprotonation by THF/DIEA. The boryl radical adds to styrene to give a benzyl radical, which is reduced at the cathode to a boronate alkyl anion that is protonated by H+ to deliver the product, with concomitant cathodic H2 evolution.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield decreased from 72% to 8% with increasing TEMPO loading; alkyl-TEMPO adduct 9 (m/z 387.3053/388.3030) isolated, confirming benzyl C-radical

probe_reagents.name: Galvinoxyl free radical
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed with 1 equiv; alkyl-galvinoxyl adduct 8 (m/z 548.4141/549.4119) detected by MS, confirming Bpin radical

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signal (g=2.0052, A_N=14.47 G, A_H=20.82 G) and MS detection of DMPO-CH2CN adduct 10 confirmed •CH2CN

probe_reagents.name: THF-d8 / CD3CN
probe_reagents.observation: No D incorporation with CH3CN/THF-d8 (THF not H source); with CD3CN/THF gave d1-3a:3a = 84:16 with D at benzyl position, identifying CH3CN as primary H donor

probe_reagents.name: d1-HBpin (DBpin)
probe_reagents.observation: DBpin + styrene gave d1-3a:3a = 16:84, indicating HBpin contributes only minor H at benzyl site

probe_reagents.name: d8-styrene
probe_reagents.observation: d8-styrene + HBpin gave d8-3a (65%), confirming Bpin originates from HBpin

probe_reagents.name: H2 evolution monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: H2 bubbles detected by GC; rate decays to zero at ~180 min matching reaction time; substrate-free runs show CH3CN+DIEA generate large H2, while THF is not a H+ source

notes: Authors note that direct quantitative detection of free Bpin radical was not possible; it may be stabilized by DIEA/THF. The lifetime/migration of the persistent benzyl radical F between electrodes is acknowledged as an open question. THF likely assists proton transfer to substrate ([THF]2H+) rather than serving as H source. With 2-vinylnaphthalene and excess HBpin (4.4 equiv), diboronate ester 7r forms selectively (71%), suggesting the benzyl radical can be further borylated rather than reduced/protonated when an extended π-system stabilizes it.
